steps.title: Identify the meaning the blank must carry
steps.content: Knowing about the Santiago Stock Exchange's regulatory oversight should have a specific effect: it removes or reduces investor worry about bureaucratic details, freeing them to focus on business opportunities. The word "thereby" signals this cause-and-effect logic. The blank needs a verb meaning to calm or reduce concerns — not to label, broadcast, or intensify them.

steps.title: Test each word against that meaning
steps.content:
Substitute each choice and check whether it fits the logic of calming investor worry:
  • Choice A — designate: to officially label or assign — knowing about oversight would "label" worries? That has no sensible meaning in context. ✗
  • Choice B — amplify: to increase or intensify — this is the opposite of what's needed; amplifying worries would prevent investors from refocusing. ✗
  • Choice C — assuage: to ease or relieve — knowing about regulatory oversight eases investors' concerns about bureaucratic minutiae, allowing them to redirect attention. ✓
  • Choice D — convey: to communicate or transmit — knowing about oversight would "transmit" worries? This neither eases nor logically interacts with concerns the way the sentence requires. ✗
That matches Choice C — assuage.
